steps.title: Introduce the contrast
steps.content: The first part makes a general claim: the two centaur objects show striking differences in behavior. The second part explains that claim by contrasting an active centaur with a dormant one.

steps.title: Combine colon and contrast
steps.content: Choice A breaks the explanation into a weaker sentence. Choice B makes the contrast a subordinate clause without properly introducing the elaboration. Choice C uses a semicolon but loses the explicit contrast signal. The remaining choice uses a colon to introduce the explanation and "while" to mark contrast. The answer is D.
